summary_sections.title: Key Concepts: Mass Wasting & Landslides
summary_sections.content_html:
Mass wasting (mass movement) is the downslope movement of rock, soil, and debris under the influence of gravity.

  • Types (by speed and material):
    • Rockfall: sudden free-fall of rock from a cliff
    • Landslide: rapid sliding of a mass of rock/soil along a slope
    • Mudflow/debris flow: rapid flow of water-saturated material
    • Creep: very slow, gradual downslope movement (tilts fences, trees, headstones)
  • Factors that increase risk: steep slopes, heavy rainfall (saturates soil), removal of vegetation, earthquakes, undercutting of slopes by erosion or construction
  • Angle of repose: the steepest angle at which loose material remains stable without sliding
